schema_notes: Per-billionaire v3 record matching ellison reference. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events canonically scoped by event_role. Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — Rupert Murdoch + regen_v3@sha256-a478ab9bce64efff

person.wealth_source: News Corp / Fox Corp founder and controlling shareholder via Murdoch Family Trust (Cruden Financial Services, sole trustee). 1999 divorce from Anna Torv converted a portion of holdings into the family trust; 2025 $3.3B settlement restructured beneficial interests.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 list shows Murdoch & family at ~$22.7B (rank #112). Bloomberg Billionaires Index and secondary aggregators show $20–24B. The 2025 succession settlement moved $3.3B out of Rupert's controlled pool: James, Elisabeth, and Prudence each received $1.1B and exited the trust's controlling stake. Forbes generally reports the family-combined figure, not Rupert-personal.

sources.fetch_note: Forbes profile page blocked to WebFetch in this environment; value triangulated from Scripps News Forbes 2026 summary and Bloomberg Billionaires Index.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Murdoch's wealth is concentrated in Fox Corp and News Corp equity held through the Murdoch Family Trust. Dual-class structure (trust owns ~14% economic / ~41% voting of News Corp) means divestment would move both stocks and threaten control — highly illiquid by design. Post-2025 settlement, Rupert personally controls a smaller beneficial share of the trust corpus (younger daughters Grace and Chloe retain one-third each; Lachlan controls voting). Apply 15% liquidity discount for immediate giving capacity, 40–50% for lifetime expected-giving denominator.

How much of the A$5M was Rupert personally vs. family/News Corp is not disclosed. Treated here as half attributable to Rupert.

cited_events.notes: Verified: Rupert Murdoch is NOT listed as a Giving Pledge signatory. No Murdoch family member on the current pledger roster. This is consistent with Forbes assigning Murdoch their lowest possible philanthropy score (1/5).

cited_events.classification_note: This is an intra-family wealth transfer to settle a trust-control lawsuit, NOT charitable giving. Included here for context because Inside Philanthropy has suggested the payouts may boost downstream family philanthropy (James/Kathryn Quadrivium has a precedent of redeploying such proceeds). Rupert's personal philanthropy was not altered by the deal. NOT counted in observable giving.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33/$1. Note: News Corp / Fox Corp editorial influence is arguably the larger political vehicle here but is not tallied as personal political spending.
political_giving.observed_total_note: OpenSecrets: ~$750K federal since 1990 (80% Republican). Plus $1M California GOP 1996. Plus $2M Senate Leadership Fund October 2024. Plus smaller cycle contributions. Total personal lifetime federal + major state: ~$3.5M through 2025. This is modest by UHNW standards — his son James alone spent $100M in the 2020 cycle via Quadrivium-linked vehicles. Rupert's institutional political influence runs through News Corp/Fox editorial, not personal checks.

foundations_dormant.name: The Murdoch Foundation Inc
foundations_dormant.status: DORMANT — dropped from IRS tax-exempt list post-2015
foundations_dormant.grants_note: Per Inside Philanthropy, foundation distributed ~$6M in the three years preceding 2008, >50% to Weill Cornell Medical School. Filed $0 grants 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015. Revenue in those years ranged from $1 to $258.
foundations_dormant.payout_rate_notes: No payout for a decade-plus. Effectively a defunct shell.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only — DAFs are donor-opaque.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC identified for Rupert personally. Family philanthropic LLCs belong to other relatives (James/Kathryn's Quadrivium Foundation is a 501(c)(3), EIN 45-3536976 — NOT Rupert's vehicle and NOT credited to him).

corporate_vehicles_not_personal.classification_note: Corporate giving from a publicly held media company. NOT counted as Rupert Murdoch's personal philanthropy under Forbes/IPS methodology — these are shareholder-funded corporate grants, not personal-wealth deployments.

corporate_vehicles_not_personal.name: Fox Corporation charitable giving
corporate_vehicles_not_personal.classification_note: Same treatment as News Corp — corporate, not personal. No direct personal attribution.

detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. The Murdoch Family Trust itself is a Nevada trust (not offshore), extensively litigated and therefore partially documented.

trusts_disclosed.name: Murdoch Family Trust (MFT) — 'In the Matter of the Doe 1 Trust'
trusts_disclosed.structure_note: Six-director board: four older children each appoint one director (one vote), Rupert appoints two directors (two votes each). Chloe and Grace (younger daughters) are non-voting beneficiaries entitled to one-third of trust assets upon turning 30. Dual-class News Corp shares held: ~14% economic, ~41% voting.
trusts_disclosed.classification_note: This is a WEALTH-CONTROL trust, not a charitable trust. Nevada 'bad faith' ruling (Judge Gorman, 9 December 2024) and $3.3B settlement (8 September 2025) are about succession, not philanthropy. No public evidence of charitable remainder or charitable-lead structure.

board_affiliations_non_financial.classification_note: Overseer roles often involve expected gifts but amounts are not disclosed. Included under hidden_upper.anonymous_cohort estimate, not observable.

board_affiliations_non_financial.institution: Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation (board member per secondary sources)

red_flags.evidence: Rupert Murdoch has not signed the Giving Pledge despite being one of the ~200 longest-tenured US+AUS billionaires. Verified against current givingpledge.org signatory roster.

The Murdoch Foundation Inc (EIN 13-3756893) has paid $0 in grants from 2008 onward through its last public 990-PF (2015), and subsequently dropped from the IRS tax-exempt list. Rupert remains Chair & President on record.

Forbes assigns Murdoch a 1/5 philanthropy score — the lowest possible — for his personal giving record across net worth, percentage given, pledge status, and foundation activity. Inside Philanthropy has repeatedly characterized him as 'Scrooge-like'.

Public-facing philanthropy (Motion Picture & Television Fund $20M, Murdoch Community Hero Awards, News Corp bushfire pledge) is corporate News Corp/Fox spending — shareholder-funded, not Rupert's personal wealth deployment. The optics of a giving empire obscure negligible personal giving.

The Murdoch Family Trust — the primary vehicle holding Rupert's wealth — is a succession/control trust, not a charitable lead or remainder trust. The 2024 Nevada case and 2025 $3.3B settlement confirm the trust's purpose is intergenerational control of Fox/News Corp, not charitable disposition.

red_flags_not_present: HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (Murdoch Foundation 990s show zero compensation for all officers including Rupert)
LOW_PAYOUT (technically the foundation is at 0% payout, but it's de-facto dormant with near-zero assets — not a live foundation hoarding assets)
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity identified — no grants at all)
POLITICAL_DOMINATES_CHARITABLE (his $3.5M lifetime personal political giving is modest; the much larger political footprint runs through News Corp/Fox editorial)

rollup.observable_giving_method: Sum of attributable-to-Rupert personal events: MCRI 1984 (~$2.1M, half attribution of A$5M family gift), Murdoch Foundation lifetime through 2008 (~$6M upper bound, possibly partly inside-family), Rick Warren P.E.A.C.E. $2M (risk of being inside the foundation bucket — dedup to avoid double count), Chelsea Pensioners $15K, Tony Blair Faith Foundation $100K, Milly Dowler $500K (half-attributed — settlement-flavored), bushfire relief $1.4M, Herald Sun 2024 $100K (half-attributed). Conservative cumulative ≈ $8M lifetime. Excludes: News Corp/Fox corporate giving (not personal); political giving; succession settlement (not charity).
rollup.observable_giving_lifetime_caveat: This is a LIFETIME-to-date number against ~35 years as a billionaire. Larry Ellison's $467M figure is 2019–2024 foundation output alone — apples-to-oranges. Murdoch's personal observable giving at ~$8M lifetime is roughly 0.04% of current net worth and is materially lower on a %-of-wealth basis than nearly every documented US billionaire we have measured.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1). liquid_wealth = 0.15 × $22.7B = $3.405B. Tenure cap saturated (35 years). Expected ≈ $340.5M. Stricter 10% formal norm.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Murdoch has a Hoover Overseer board seat and Reagan Foundation affiliation with plausible but undisclosed gifts. We model an anonymous cohort uplift of ~0.4% of liquid_wealth = $15M as a generous upper bound. Even at this uplift, total observable+hidden < 7% of the 10% expected norm.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Rick Warren P.E.A.C.E. $2M gift suggests some evangelical-adjacent giving, but no pattern of tithing. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Unlike Ellison, Murdoch has NO disclosed charitable-intent trust. The Murdoch Family Trust is explicitly a succession/control vehicle; the 2025 settlement confirms it distributes to heirs, not charity. No trust-outcome uncertainty to model.
hidden_upper_usd.news_corp_corporate_note: News Corp Foundation / Fox Corp corporate giving is excluded from hidden upper — it is shareholder-funded and not attributable to Rupert personally. Including it would double-count public-company dollars.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Murdoch: ($8M + $15M) / $340.5M = 6.76% — well below 0.2 threshold. Unlike Ellison, he has no Giving Pledge commitment, no disclosed charitable trust, and no philanthropic LLC that would force us up to 'Probably Low'. The evidence is consistent and convergent: Forbes' lowest philanthropy score, a dormant foundation, no pledge, no announced major gifts in 20+ years. This is a canonical Tier A Verified Low case.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Murdoch as the canonical counterexample to the 'billionaires quietly give a lot' narrative: his personal philanthropy is vanishingly small in both absolute and percentage terms, his foundation is dormant, and he has declined the Giving Pledge. The large News Corp/Fox corporate giving footprint is not his personal wealth. The $3.3B 2025 settlement moved $1.1B into each of three children's hands — family downstream giving (especially Quadrivium) may grow, but that is not Rupert's giving.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: No pledge breach (he never signed). Maximum tenure penalty (35 years). Near-maximum giving gap (observable 0.035% of NW). 5 red flags. Computed for internal continuity. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — publish tiers only per PLAN.md.
